1: Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise;
2: They have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
3: They compassed me about also with words of hatred; And fought against me without a cause.
4: But I give myself unto give myself unto prayer.
6: And let Satan stand at his right hand.
7: And let his prayer become sin.
8: And And let another take his office.
10: Let them seek their bread their bread also out of their desolate places.
11: And let the strangers spoil his labour.
12: Neither let there be any to favour his fatherless children.
13: And And in the generation following let their name be blotted out.
14: And let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
15: That he may cut off the memory of them from the earth.
16: But persecuted the poor and needy man, That he might even slay the broken in heart.
17: As he delighted not in blessing, so let it be far from him.
18: So let it come into his bowels like water, and like oil into his bones.
19: And for a girdle wherewith he is girded continually.
20: And of them that speak evil against my soul.
21: Because thy mercy is is good, deliver thou me.
22: And my heart is wounded within me.
23: I am tossed up and down as the locust.
24: And my flesh faileth of fatness.
25: When When they looked upon me they shaked their heads.
26: O save me according to thy mercy:
27: That That thou, LORD LORD , hast done it.
28: When they arise, let them be ashamed; but let thy servant rejoice.
29: And let them cover themselves with their own confusion, as with a mantle.
30: Yea, I will praise him among the multitude.
31: To save him him from those that condemn his soul.
